verdict_reasoning: C3 is a secreted protein synthesized with a signal peptide and no transmembrane domain. While C3b (its activated cleavage product) covalently attaches to pathogen and damaged-cell surfaces via a reactive thioester, C3 itself is a plasma protein with no intrinsic membrane anchor. Surface C3b deposition is a recruitment mechanism (complement activation enzymatically deposits C3b onto targets), not evidence of C3's own surface accessibility. Intracellular C3 pools are cytosolic or endosomal; no documented outer-leaflet display in baseline or stress states. No documented stable TM-partner tethering, no tissue-restricted surface lineage, no lysosomal exocytosis-dependent display, no documented activation-induced ecto-presentation, and no documented cancer/cell-death-induced surface translocation.
